description_long: This fence is constructed from horizontally stacked, weathered wooden planks, each showing significant signs of age, such as peeling paint and rough, uneven surfaces. The planks are arranged tightly together, forming a solid barrier with no visible gaps between them. The coloration is a mix of faded reds, browns, and grays, suggesting prolonged exposure to the elements and a rustic, utilitarian design. The top edge of the fence is irregular and jagged, following the natural wear and tear of the wood rather than a clean cut. There are no visible posts, caps, or decorative elements, giving the fence a simple, functional appearance. This type of fence is commonly found in older urban or industrial areas, serving as a boundary or privacy barrier.
description: A weathered, horizontal wooden plank fence with peeling paint and a rough, jagged top edge. The coloration is a mix of faded reds, browns, and grays, giving it a rustic, utilitarian look.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full face of the fence with horizontal planks, visible weathering, and a jagged top edge.
description_view_1: Side profile view, revealing the fence's very thin depth and the stacked arrangement of the planks.
description_view_2: Rear view, nearly identical to the front, displaying the same horizontal planks and weathered texture.
description_view_3: Opposite side profile, again showing the thinness and layered structure of the fence.
